caption.full:
Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey' leads practical filmmaking trend

Recent box office hits show a preference for practical effects over CGI-heavy franchises. Films like 'The Odyssey', 'Project Hail Mary', 'Backrooms', and 'Obsession' are relying on old-school filmmaking techniques. Christopher Nolan refused to use CGI for set extensions in 'The Odyssey', stating the film needed to feel difficult.

Which director’s commitment to practical filmmaking do you think redefined the industry?

AI-assisted recap by The Screen Report.

#BoxOffice #Hollywood #TheOdyssey #ChristopherNolan

youtube.title: Christopher Nolan: 'The Odyssey' Filmed Without CGI
youtube.description:
Christopher Nolan refused to use CGI for set extensions in 'The Odyssey,' stating, "We're not doing the job right making a film of The Odyssey if it doesn't seem difficult." Recent box office hits show a preference for practical effects and old-school filmmaking over CGI-heavy franchises, with films like 'Project Hail Mary' and 'Obsession' also relying on practical effects.
